Question: Algebra uses letters to represent numbers.
A variable represents an unknown value.
A constant is a fixed number.
An equation has an equal sign.
The coefficient of x in 5x is 5
The coefficient of y in -3y is -3
The coefficient of x in x is 1
The additive inverse of x is -x
The sum of a number and its additive inverse is 0
Solving equations is not part of algebra.
Algebra helps in solving real-life problems.
A mixed expression can have numbers and variables.
An equation always has one solution.
Equations can have more than one solution.
x² - 4 = 0 has solutions x = 2 or x = -2
x² + 2x + 1 = 0 has a single solution x = -1
